We recommended that:
5. Municipal Treasurer acknowledged the said trust through issuance of an official receipt and deposit the same to their Trust Fund account; and
6. Municipal Accountant to record the subject Trust Fund in their book of accounts.

7. We recommended that Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to prepare and formulate development plans and projects aligned with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of DILG and DBM to include in the deliberation of the Sanggunian Bayan during the preparation of the budget and to appropriate funds out of the 20% economic development funds of the Internal Revenue Allotment of the municipal government.

1.6 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to duly recognize the monthly depreciation of the above PPE
in order to reflect the correct carrying amounts.

2.8 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to properly recognize all procurements of inventory items
under the Inventory account, except those purchased through the petty
cash fund, following the perpetual inventory method and to properly
maintain SLC pursuant to Manual on NGAS.

3.5 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to facilitate the preparation and prompt submission of the
monthly BRS to the Audit Team for their review, and as required under
Section 74 P.D. No. 1445 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 96-011. In
case of delayed production of the bank statements, securing snapshots as
of a given date will do.

4.5 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the submission of
POs/contract documents not later than five (5) working days from
perfection of contract together with duly accomplished supporting
documents and the AIRs within 24 hours from acceptance of goods, in
compliance with COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006.

5.6 We recommended and Management agreed to require the BAC
Secretariat in collaboration with all concerned key offices to facilitate the
preparation and submission of a comprehensive, workable and realistic
APP pursuant to Section 7 Rule II of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

6.3 We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and submit the Report on LDRRMF every
month, whether there is transaction or utilization for the period, as
required by COA Circular No. 2012-002.

1. We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee, composed of a representative from the Mayor’s Office, the General Services Office/or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and in other departments and conduct the required annual physical count of PPE of the municipality.

2. We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Head of General Services Office, in coordination with the Municipal Social Worker for Development Office to prepare and submit the RSMI and RIS to the Municipal Accountant to support the credits made to account Welfare Goods for Distribution.

3. We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the separate Trial Balance for the Special accounts in the General Fund, most importantly for development projects funded out of IRA that will facilitate reporting of funds utilized and review thereof.

4. We recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal Person to prepare and submit the GAD accomplishment report accompanied with its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for validation and tracking of the resources on GAD focused policies, programs and projects based on the approved GAD Plan pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

8. We recommended that Implementing Agency (IA) to which the funds are transferred for the purpose of implementing the project should perform the duties and responsibilities enumerated in item 6 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

1.5 We recommended that Management require the concerned officials to
submit the above required documents for the said disbursements for
evaluation and further verification of Audit Team.

2.5 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant
to prepare and submit the required Bank Reconciliation Statements
and reclassify and/ or adjust affected transaction accounts paid in cash
where Cash -in- Bank had been already credited but checks issued
thereon were cancelled and/or not presented for payment as of
December 31, 2018 to Accounts Payable.

2.6 We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor also to suspend
payment of salary of all responsible officers tasked to prepare bank
reconciliation until said statement is made.

3.3 We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer prepare and formulate
development plans and projects aligned with the Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of DILG and DBM to include in
the deliberation of the Sanggunian Bayan during the preparation of the
budget and to appropriate funds out of the 20% economic development
funds of the Internal Revenue Allotment of the municipal government.

4.5 We recommended that Management initiate the conduct of a complete
physical inventory of the municipality’s properties by creating an
Inventory Committee composed of a representative each from the
Mayor’s Office, from the General Services Office (or the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office) and a representative from each department to
prepare the necessary procedures in the proper and orderly conduct of
the physical count of the all the property, plant and equipment and
inventories owned by the Municipality.

5.5 We recommended that Management instruct the concerned officials to
establish and maintain a GAD database containing Sex-Disaggregated
data and gender statistics and include it in the preparation of the GAD
Planning and Budget in compliance with PCW/NEDA/DBM Joint
Circular No. 2012-01.

6.4 We recommended that Management follow strictly the guidelines set
under R.A. 10121, the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and
management Act of 2010, on the transfer to the Trust Fund of the
unutilized balance of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Fund.

7.6 We recommended that Management as the Implementing Agency (IA)
perform the duties and responsibilities enumerated in item 6 of COA
Circular No. 94-013.

Recommendation:
We recommend that  the Municipal Treasurer and  the Municipal Accountant shall record and post in the Cashbook and the books of accounts respectively the fund transfer amounting to P398,000.00 and adjust the cash and cash equivalent accounts so as to present the financial statements of the agency fairly presented and free from material misstatements. Furthermore, strictly observe the procedures and process of collections and deposits of income and revenue.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management strictly require the preparation and approval of the PO before any payment is made on the purchase of construction materials for projects implemented by the Municipal Government in compliance on the above COA Circular.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal  Accountant  be directed to cause the immediate submission of the Report of  Collections  and  Deposits covering  the  P131,955.00  funds  received/collected  during  the  year   2017   together  with  the supporting official receipts and validated deposit slips.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management initiate the conduct of a complete Physical inventory of the municipality’s properties by creating an Inventory Committee composed of representative each from the Mayor’s Office, from the General Services Office (or the Municipal Treasurer’s Office) and a representative from each department to prepare the necessary procedures in the proper and orderly conduct of the physical count of the all the property, plant and equipment and inventories owned by the Municipality.

Recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the Supply Officer to record all purchases of office supplies to the Office Supplies Inventory account, Food supplies Inventory and Drugs and Medicines inventory account as provided under the Perpetual Inventory Method of accounting for inventories.
The Supply Officer should be instructed to prepare the RSMI for all issuances of office supplies and submit the same to the Accounting Office, on a monthly basis for the preparation of the JEV to take up the expense account.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipality shall ensure that the Accountant prepares the Report on LDRRMF every month whether there is transaction/ utilization for that month as required by COA Circular No. 2012-002.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the concerned officials of the Municipality establish and maintain a GAD database containing sex-Disaggregated  data and gender statistics and include it in the preparation of the GAD Planning and Budget in compliance with PCW/NEDA/ DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

We recommend that the
Provincial Treasurer of Lanao
del Sur shall cause the
official documentation of the
turnover of offices between and
among reshuffled or OIC and the
replaced officers with the
preparation of invoices and
receipt of property and
responsibility accountabilities
to determine properly their
respective responsibilities and
accountabilities. The usual
clearance of outgoing officials
shall also ordered to ensure
that such officials or
employees has no pending
accounts or if there is any,
that proper accounting thereof
has been made. Allegation of
late submission of this
required documents should not
be given credence considering
that the agency is a public
office and not a personal
property of any of the
officials concerned.

We recommend that the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant be directed to cause
the immediate submission of the
Report of Collections and
Deposits covering the
P175,372.00 funds
received/collected during the
year 2015 together with the
supporting official receipts
and validated deposit slips.

Initiate the conduct of a
complete physical inventory of
the \municipality’s properties
by     creating       an       
Inventory      Committee       
composed      of       a       
representative each from the
Mayor’s Office, from the
General Services Office (or the
Municipal Treasurer’s Office)  
and a representative from each
department to prepare the
necessary procedures in the
proper and orderly conduct of
the physical count of the all
the property, plant and
equipment and inventories owned
by the Municipality;

Require the Inventory Committee
to conduct the physical count of
all the properties recorded
in the books and those not yet
recorded but actually found in
the LGU offices/premises and to
prepare the corresponding
inventory reports thereof; and
reconcile them with the
accounting records to come up
with the correct account
balances in the financial
statement.

Require the Municipal Treasurer
and Municipal Accountant to
maintain stock, property and
ledger cards, respectively to
properly account the receipt
and disposition of property and
supplies.

We recommended that officials concerned were advise to comply within 90 days with the requirements of the transactions suspended in audit and submit lacking supporting papers.  Otherwise, it will be disallowed in audit and settlement thereof should be immediately enforced by withholding the salaries or other claims of the persons liable in satisfaction of the amounts disallowed which is final and executory.

We recommend that management should ensure that due notification to the Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled delivery of goods and services be made on time pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant be directed to cause the immediate submission of the Report of Collections and Deposits covering the P387,759.04 funds received/collected during the year 2016 together with the supporting official receipts and validated deposit slips.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to faithfully observe Sections 29-34, Volume I and Section 51 Volume II of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System and require all collecting officers to submit reports of accountability for accountable forms and prepare thereon a consolidated report of accountability for accountable forms on a periodic basis, i.e., monthly.  We recommend also that Management designate regular personnel who will take charge of the consolidation and preparation of the CRAAF duly verified by the Municipal Treasurer to ensure prompt submission of the necessary monthly Report of Collections and Deposits.  Enforce all designated collectors finally to remit daily their collections intact.

Encourage and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management system and constitute the Municipality Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2) Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

It is recommended that the management should widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.

We recommend that the previous administration of the municipality shall cause the official documentation of the turnover of offices between and among the outgoing and the incoming officials of the municipality with the preparation of invoices and receipt of property and responsibility accountabilities to determine property their respective responsibilities.

We recommend that management in
the succeeding fiscal years,
shall include in the Annual
Appropriations for Senior
citizens  and persons wit
disabilities and 2% for the
implementation of the programs
of the Local Council for the
protection of children as
required under RA 9442 amending
RA 7277 and 9344 so that PAPS
for them can be perfectly
executed.

Initiate measures designed to
come up with a Memorandum of
Agreement (MOA) with the GSIS
defining therein the agreed
balance of payables to GSIS, if
any, and the terms and
conditions in the settlement
thereof.  The following steps
may help in facilitating
reconciliation of records.

Secure detailed profile of
payables per GSIS records;
Reconstruct old GSIS accounts,
if necessary, and update
records;
Reconstruct old personnel
profile, if necessary, and
update personnel profile;
Counter-check GSIS records with
the reconstructed and updated
accounting and personnel
records of the LGU;
Negotiate with the GSIS for any
differences that may be noted
during counter-checking
activity;
Enter into a formal agreement
with the GSIS in the form of a
Memorandum of Agreement (MOA);
and Appropriate fund for the
settlement of any payables to
the GSIS as agreed in the MOA.

We recommend to the Local Chief
Executive (LCE) to direct the
personnel concerned to settle
their respective Notice of
Suspension Pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2009-006 dated
September 15, 2009.
